data.title: Customers Bancorp Q3 net income rises as NIM expands
data.description:
Overview

  • Customers Bancorp Q3 net income rises to $73.7 mln, reflecting strong financial performance

  • Company's net interest margin increases to 3.46%, driven by higher interest income

  • Customers Bancorp raises $163 mln in common equity, boosting capital strength

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN - Increase driven by higher interest income from loans and leases and higher average non-interest bearing deposit balances

  • CAPITAL STRENGTH - $163 mln common equity raise bolsters capital position

  • LOAN GROWTH - Specialized lending and consumer installment loans see significant increases

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q3 Core EPS

$2.20

Q3 Net Income

$73.72 mln

Q3 CET1 Capital Ratio

13%

Q3 Core ROCE

15.52%

Q3 ROCE

15.57%

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy", 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Customers Bancorp Inc is $78.50, about 17.2% above its October 21 closing price of $64.97

  • The stock recently traded at 9 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 9 three months ago

Press Release:

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: PRES TRUMP Q&A: COST OF BALLROOM MAY BE UP TO $350 MILLION; 'WILL DONATE WHATEVER'S NEEDED;' CAN'T SAY WHAT'S DONATED SO FAR
Deckers Outdoor Corp Bottom Line Rises In Q2
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Deckers Outdoor Corp (DECK) revealed a profit for its second quarter that Increased, from the same period last year

The company's bottom line totaled $268.15 million, or $1.82 per share. This compares with $242.32 million, or $1.59 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 9.2% to $1.43 billion from $1.31 billion last year.

Deckers Outdoor Corp earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $268.15 Mln. vs. $242.32 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $1.82 vs. $1.59 last year.

-Revenue: $1.43 Bln vs. $1.31 Bln last year.

<b>-Guidance</b>:

Full year EPS guidance: $6.30 - $6.39

data.title: Boston Beer Boosts FY Profit Outlook, Sees Lower Tariff Risk
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Boston Beer raised its profit guidance for the year as it sees a milder tariff headwind.

The brewer, which owns the Samuel Adams and Truly brands, now forecasts earnings per share of $7.80 to $9.80 for the year, up from its prior outlook of $6.72 to $9.54. Both ranges include added tariff costs.

The company now expects tariffs to add about $9 million to $13 million in costs for the year, down from its prior view of $15 million to $20 million.

The updated guidance comes after the company logged lower revenue in its latest quarter as depletions and shipments fell.

Boston Beer posted a profit of $46.2 million, or $4.25 a share, for the third quarter, compared with $33.5 million, or $2.86 a share, a year earlier. The year-ago quarter included a non-cash brand impairment charge of $29.1 million.

Analysts expected earnings of $3.33 a share, according to FactSet.

Net revenue, which excludes excise taxes, slid 11% to $537.5 million. Analysts expected $542.1 million.

Depletions, a key metric that tracks sales by distributors to retailers and is considered a proxy for demand, declined 3% as volumes were pressured across the beer industry, Chief Executive Officer Jim Koch said.

Koch, who returned as CEO in August, said the company is encouraged by positive reactions to its Sun Cruiser brand innovation and a second consecutive quarter of growth for its Angry Orchard business.

"We are continuing to invest in advertising and innovation across our portfolio of brands to grow market share and position ourselves well for when the consumer environment improves," Koch said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Correction to Thermo Fisher Article on Oct. 22
data.description: "Thermo Scientific 3Q Revenue Rises, Boosted by Life Sciences Business" published at 6:28 a.m. ET on Oct. 22, 2025 misstated the name of the company in the headline and first paragraph as Thermo Scientific instead of Thermo Fisher.

data.title: Applied Materials to Cut 4% of Global Workforce
data.description:
By Josée Rose

Applied Materials is cutting about 4% of its global workforce, effective immediately.

The semiconductor company said it is taking one coordinated workforce action as "automation, digitalization and geographic shifts are redefining our workforce needs and skill requirements," according to a letter to employees, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday.

The company has started notifying employees as of Oct. 23, the letter said.

Applied Materials expects to incur charges of about $160 million to $180 million related to the terminations, and will recognize most of that cost in the fourth quarter. The plan should be completed in the fiscal-first quarter of 2026.

The company plans to continue its focus on "building high-velocity, high-productivity teams, adopting new technologies and simplifying organizational structures" and wants to be "better positioned to scale effectively," according to the letter.

Applied Materials had 36,100 regular full-time employees as of July 27, according to its latest earnings release from August.

Write to Josée Rose at josee.rose@wsj.com

data.title: Western Union Co Q3 Profit Decreases, But Beats Estimates
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Western Union Co (WU) reported earnings for third quarter that Decreased from the same period last year but beat the Street estimates.

The company's bottom line came in at $139.6 million, or $0.43 per share. This compares with $264.8 million, or $0.78 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Western Union Co reported adjusted earnings of $151.2 million or $0.47 per share for the period.

Analysts on average had expected the company to earn $0.43 per share. Analysts' estimates typically exclude special items.

The company's revenue for the period fell 0.4% to $1.032 billion from $1.036 billion last year.

Western Union Co earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $139.6 Mln. vs. $264.8 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.43 vs. $0.78 last year.

-Revenue: $1.032 Bln vs. $1.036 Bln last year.

<b>-Guidance</b>:

Full year EPS guidance: $1.65 - $1.75

Full year revenue guidance: $4.085 - $4.185 Bln

data.title: CEO of eXoZymes to Present at ThinkEquity's Annual Investor Conference
data.description:
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA / ACCESS Newswire / October 23, 2025 / Today, eXoZymes Inc. ("eXoZymes") - a pioneer of AI-engineered enzymes that can transform sustainable feedstock into nutraceuticals, medicines, and other essential chemicals - announced that CEO, Michael Heltzen, will present at the 2025 ThinkEquity Conference on October 30, 2025, at the Mandarin Oriental Hotel in New York City.

CEO of eXoZymes, Michael Heltzen, states, "Most investors already know that - compared to the number of dollars invested - synthetic biology unfortunately has very little to show for it because it never scaled commercially. That's why we have introduced the next generation of biomanufacturing - a groundbreaking new way of making chemicals by using AI to convert enzymes to function cell-free in a bioreactor. Our initial focus with this paradigm shifting platform is natural products with the potential to become pharmaceuticals. Leading this strategy with a natural product called NCT which is showing very promising results in improving metabolic health."

eXoZymes has developed a biomanufacturing platform that - as a historic first - offers the tools and insights to design, engineer, control and optimize nature's own natural processes to produce chemical compounds, enabling the company's partners to upgrade traditional petro-chemical production methods - or natural extraction - with a new commercially scalable, sustainable, and eco-friendly alternative: exozymes.

Heltzen continues, "By using the ingenuity of biology, coupled with AI, and combining it with the scalability of chemical engineering - we have solved the scaling bottleneck of synthetic biology. As such, we believe eXoZymes is positioned to lead the 4th industrial revolution at the intersection of biology and technology, which we're excited to detail to investors at ThinkEquity's conference next week!"

Organized by ThinkEquity LLC, the conference will showcase more than 85 carefully selected companies across biotechnology, artificial intelligence, crypto, mining, and other high-growth sectors. Over 750 institutional and high-net-worth investors are expected to attend.

Michael Heltzen will be presenting eXoZymes to investors on Thursday, October 30, at 8:00-8:30 AM in the Lotus Suite East at the Mandarin Oriental Hotel.

About ThinkEquity LLC

ThinkEquity is a boutique investment bank created by experienced professionals that have worked together for over a decade, collectively financing over $50 billion of public and private capital raises, restructurings, and mergers and acquisitions.

For more information about ThinkEquity, visit https://www.think-equity.com

About eXoZymes

Founded in 2019, the company has developed a biomanufacturing platform that - as a historic first - offers the tools and insights to design, engineer, control and optimize nature's own natural processes to produce chemical compounds, enabling the company's partners to upgrade traditional petro-chemical production methods with a new commercially scalable, sustainable, and eco-friendly alternative: exozymes.

Exozymes are advanced enzymes enhanced through AI and bioengineering to thrive in a bioreactor without using living cells. Exozymes can replace toxic petrochemical processes and inefficient biochemical extraction with sustainable and scalable biosolutions that transform biomass into essential chemicals, medicines, and biofuels.

By freeing enzyme-driven chemical reactions from the limitations imposed by cells, exozyme biosolutions eliminate the scaling bottleneck that has hampered commercial success in the synthetic biology (SynBio) space, making exozymes the next generation of biomanufacturing.

While the company, eXoZymes Inc., has introduced "exozymes" as a scientific concept, they are not trademarking the concept, as they view it as a new nomenclature for wide adoption for this next generation of biomanufacturing that eXoZymes aims to pioneer and be the market leader of.

Learn more on exozymes.com

eXoZymes Safe Harbor

This press release includes forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements, which are based on certain assumptions and describe the company's future plans, strategies and expectations, can generally be identified by the use of forward-looking terms such as "believe," "expect," "may," "will," "should," "would," "could," "seek," "intend," "plan," "goal," "project," "estimate," "anticipate," "strategy," "future," "likely," "potential," or other comparable terms, although not all forward-looking statements contain these identifying words. All statements other than statements of historical facts included in this press release regarding the company's strategies, prospects, financial condition, operations, costs, plans and objectives are forward-looking statements. Actual results could differ materially for a variety of reasons. You should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described in the "Risk Factors" section of eXoZymes' quarterly reports on Form 10-Q, annual reports on Form 10-K, and other documents filed by eXoZymes from time to time by the company with the Securities and Exchange Commission. These filings identify and address important risks and uncertainties that could cause actual events and results to differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made. Readers are cautioned not to put undue reliance on forward-looking statements, and eXoZymes assumes no obligation and does not intend to update or revise these forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events, or otherwise. eXoZymes does not give any assurance that it will achieve its expectations.

data.title: Anthropic to use 1 million Google Cloud TPU chips to train its Claude chatbot
data.description:
Anthropic said on Thursday it Claude models would be trained using up to one million of Google Cloud's tensor processing units, worth tens of billions of dollars.

The deal underscores the immense computing demands of AI firms for training, deployment and continuous inference in generative AI, as they race to launch new products and features to stay ahead in a fiercely competitive market.

To secure infrastructure swiftly, developers have been rushing into multi-billion dollar agreements .

Alphabet's Google, also a backer of Anthropic, will provide additional cloud computing services to the startup behind the Claude chatbot.

data.title: VERISIGN INC/CA SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Verisign Inc., a global leader in domain name registry services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational efficiency, driven by increased domain name registrations and price adjustments for .com and .net domains. The company continues to demonstrate robust performance across various metrics, reflecting its strong market position and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $419.1 million, a 7% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by .com and .net price increases and an expanded domain name base.
  • Operating Income: $284.3 million, representing a 6% increase from the same period in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $212.8 million, up from $201.3 million in the same period in 2024, indicating strong profitability.
  • Basic Earnings per Share (EPS): $2.28, compared to $2.07 in the same period in 2024, reflecting higher net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $2.27, compared to $2.07 in the same period in 2024, showing improved earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company experienced revenue growth across all regions during the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025. The majority of growth was generated from registrars based in the U.S. and EMEA. U.S. revenues increased by 6% and 5% for the three and nine months, respectively, while EMEA saw a 13% and 12% increase.
  • Sales Units: As of September 30, 2025, Verisign had 171.9 million .com and .net registrations in the domain name base, representing a 1.4% increase from the previous year. During the three months ended September 30, 2025, 10.6 million new domain name registrations were processed, up from 9.3 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Renewal Rates: The final .com and .net renewal rate for the second quarter of 2025 was 75.5%, an improvement from 72.7% in the second quarter of 2024, indicating stronger customer retention.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company reported an increase in operating income by 6% and 5% for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: Verisign plans to continue paying a quarterly cash dividend, subject to market conditions and board approval, and has a significant amount remaining for future share repurchases under its current authorization.

SEC Filing: VERISIGN INC/CA [ VRSN ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: SLM Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
SLM Corp, a leading provider of private education loans, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic focus on maximizing profitability and growth within its core private student loan business.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $658 million, increased by $6 million or 1% compared to the same period last year, driven by a $1.8 billion increase in average Private Education Loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $373 million, increased by $14 million or 4% from the previous year, primarily due to an 18-basis point increase in net interest margin.
  • Net Income (Loss) Attributable to SLM Corporation Common Stock: $132 million, compared to a net loss of $50 million in the same quarter last year, reflecting improved interest income and gains on loan sales.
  • Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share: $0.63, compared to a loss of $0.23 per share in the prior year, reflecting the turnaround in net income.
  • Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans, Net: $136 million, resulting from a $1.94 billion Private Education Loan sale during the quarter, with no comparable gains in the prior year.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $179 million, decreased by $92 million or 34% from the previous year, primarily due to negative provisions from loan sales.
  • Total Non-Interest Income: $173 million, increased by $149 million from the previous year, driven by gains on loan sales and securities.
  • Total Non-Interest Expenses: $180 million, increased by $9 million or 5% from the previous year, primarily due to increased marketing spend.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's primary focus remains on maximizing the profitability and growth of its core private student loan business. The Private Education Loan portfolio is a significant driver of net interest income, with a notable increase in average Private Education Loans compared to the previous year.
  • Sales Units: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, the company originated $2.9 billion in Private Education Loans, an increase from $2.8 billion in the same period of 2024. The nine months ended September 30, 2025, saw total originations of $6.4 billion, up from $6.0 billion in the prior year.
  • Future Outlook: Management has provided guidance for 2025, focusing on sustaining credit quality and maintaining cost-efficient funding sources to support loan originations. The company is also committed to enhancing its risk management framework and assessing enterprise-wide risk.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is advancing its strategic imperatives by focusing on its core private student loan business, optimizing its brand, and maintaining a rigorous capital allocation program to create stockholder value.
  • Risk Management Enhancements: The company is strengthening its risk and compliance functions and enhancing its risk management framework to better manage enterprise-wide risk.
  • Loan Portfolio Management: The company sold $1.94 billion in Private Education Loans during the third quarter of 2025, which significantly impacted the provision for credit losses and resulted in gains on loan sales.

SEC Filing: SLM Corp [ SLM ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: MOODYS CORP /DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Moody's Corporation, a global leader in credit ratings, research, and risk analysis, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report highlights significant financial growth and robust operational performance across its key business segments, Moody's Analytics (MA) and Moody's Investors Service (MIS).

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $2,007 million, reflecting a year-over-year increase driven by growth in both MA and MIS segments.

Operating Income: $917 million, an improvement from the previous year, indicating enhanced operational efficiency.

Net Income: $647 million, up from $534 million in the prior year, showcasing strong profitability.

Net Income Attributable to Moody's: $646 million, demonstrating consistent performance with a focus on core business strengths.

Basic EPS: $3.61, compared to $2.94 in the previous year, highlighting improved earnings per share.

Diluted EPS: $3.60, up from $2.93, reflecting the company's ability to generate higher earnings for shareholders.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Moody's Analytics (MA): Reported revenue from Decision Solutions, Research and Insights, and Data and Information. Decision Solutions revenue was driven by Banking, Insurance, and KYC services, with notable growth in Insurance and KYC. Research and Insights and Data and Information also contributed significantly to the MA segment's performance.
  • Moody's Investors Service (MIS): Saw revenue contributions from Corporate Finance, Structured Finance, Financial Institutions, and Public, Project and Infrastructure Finance. Corporate Finance was the largest contributor, with strong performance in investment-grade and high-yield sectors.

Geographical Performance

  • U.S. Market: Remains a significant contributor to Moody's revenue, with substantial contributions from both MA and MIS segments.
  • Non-U.S. Markets: Particularly EMEA and Asia-Pacific, showed strong performance, contributing to the overall growth in revenue.
  • EMEA Region: In the MA segment, EMEA was the largest non-U.S. contributor. In the MIS segment, EMEA also led the non-U.S. revenue.
  • Asia-Pacific Region: Showed robust growth in both MA and MIS segments.

Future Outlook

Moody's continues to focus on expanding its integrated risk assessment solutions and enhancing its product offerings across both MA and MIS segments. The company is also investing in strategic growth areas and operational efficiencies to drive future performance.

SEC Filing: MOODYS CORP /DE/ [ MCO ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: KAISER ALUMINUM CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kaiser Aluminum Corp, a leading producer of semi-fabricated specialty aluminum products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by strategic pricing, cost management, and capacity expansions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $843.5 million, reflecting a 13% increase compared to the same quarter last year, driven by a 22% increase in the average realized sales price per pound.
  • Operating income: $48.8 million, a significant increase from $13.2 million in the prior year quarter, primarily due to improved pricing and cost management.
  • Net income: $39.5 million, up from $8.8 million in the previous year, reflecting strong operational performance and cost efficiencies.
  • Net income per common share, Diluted: $2.38, compared to $0.54 in the prior year, indicating improved profitability and earnings growth.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported a breakdown of net sales by end market applications for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, with Aero/HS Products contributing $182.2 million, Packaging $393.9 million, GE Products $192.2 million, and Automotive Extrusions $75.2 million. This reflects a significant increase in Packaging and GE Products sales compared to the previous year.
  • Sales Units: For the quarter ended September 30, 2025, the company shipped 270.2 million pounds of products, a decrease from 292.2 million pounds in the same period of 2024. This decline was primarily due to a planned partial outage at the Trentwood facility.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the start-up of the fourth coating line at the Warrick facility, which is expected to enhance production capacity and efficiency.
  • Operational Performance: The company experienced a decrease in manufacturing costs by $49.4 million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to favorable metal consumption and lower shipment volume.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued focus on strategic investments and capacity expansions, particularly in the Aero/HS Products and Packaging segments, to drive future growth and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: KAISER ALUMINUM CORP [ KALU ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: BOSTON BEER CO INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Boston Beer Co Inc, a leading player in the craft beer industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and future outlooks. Despite facing challenges in sales volume, the company has shown resilience through improved cost management and strategic operational efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $537.5 million, a decrease of 11.2% compared to the same period last year due to decreased sales volume, partially offset by increased pricing and favorable product mix.
  • Gross Profit: $273.1 million, representing a gross margin of 50.8%, with improvements driven by contract renegotiations, recipe optimization savings, and improved brewery efficiencies.
  • Operating Income: $62.0 million, increased by 35.4% from the prior year, reflecting lower operating expenses and improved cost management.
  • Net Income: $46.2 million, increased by 37.7% from the prior year, benefiting from lower cost of goods sold and reduced impairment charges.
  • Net Income per Common Share – Basic: $4.25, up from $2.87 in the prior year, reflecting improved profitability and cost efficiencies.

Business Highlights

  • Sales Units: The company experienced a 13.7% decrease in total shipment volume to 1,936,000 barrels for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, compared to 2,243,000 barrels in the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to reduced sales in Twisted Tea, Truly Hard Seltzer, and Samuel Adams brands, partially offset by growth in Sun Cruiser and Angry Orchard brands.
  • Sales Units: For the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, total shipment volume decreased by 3.7% to 5,776,000 barrels, compared to 5,997,000 barrels in the same period in 2024, mainly due to declines in Twisted Tea and Truly Hard Seltzer brands.
  • Revenue Segments: Shipments to domestic distributors accounted for 93% of revenue for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, while international shipments contributed 5%, and sales at retail locations made up 2%.
  • Revenue Segments: For the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, domestic shipments represented 94% of revenue, international shipments 5%, and retail sales 1%.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company achieved cost savings through contract renegotiations and recipe optimization, resulting in a decrease in cost of goods sold per barrel by 5.8% for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, and by 6.0% for the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025.
  • Advertising and Promotion: Advertising, promotional, and selling expenses increased by 11.3% for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, and by 12.0% for the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, primarily due to increased brand media and local marketing investments.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates incurring approximately $31 million in shortfall fees related to production agreements, with $14 million expected to be expensed in the remainder of 2025 and $17 million in future years, primarily in 2026.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects tariffs to have an unfavorable cost impact for the full year 2025 of approximately $9 to $13 million, affecting gross margins by 40 to 60 basis points.

SEC Filing: BOSTON BEER CO INC [ SAM ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: AUTONATION, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
AutoNation, Inc., a leading automotive retailer in the United States, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance. The report highlights significant growth in revenue, profitability, and strategic business initiatives, reflecting the company's strong market position and effective management strategies.

Financial Highlights

AutoNation, Inc. reported impressive financial results for the latest quarter, driven by higher sales across various segments and effective cost management. Key financial metrics include:

  • Total Revenue: $7,037.4 million, reflecting a year-over-year increase driven by higher sales in new and used vehicles, as well as parts and service.
  • Gross Profit: $1,238.4 million, showing an improvement due to increased revenue and effective cost management.
  • Operating Income: $372.4 million, indicating strong operational performance despite increased selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income: $215.1 million, up from the previous year, supported by higher revenue and improved interest margins.
  • Diluted EPS: $5.65, reflecting the company's ability to generate higher earnings per share through effective financial management and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

AutoNation, Inc. continues to demonstrate strong operational performance across its various business segments. Key business highlights include:

  • Revenue Segments: AutoNation's revenue is disaggregated by major lines of goods and services, including new vehicles, used vehicles, parts and service, finance and insurance, and other categories. The company operates across Domestic, Import, Premium Luxury, and Corporate segments, with each segment contributing to the total revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates 323 new vehicle franchises from 244 stores located predominantly in major metropolitan markets in the Sunbelt region of the United States. Approximately 65% of total retail new vehicle unit sales were generated by stores in Florida, California, and Texas.
  • Sales Units: The core brands of new vehicles sold, representing approximately 89% of the new vehicles sold during the nine months ended September 30, 2025, include Toyota (including Lexus), Honda, Ford, General Motors, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Stellantis, and Volkswagen (including Audi and Porsche).
  • New Product Launches: AutoNation offers a diversified range of automotive products and services, including new vehicles, used vehicles, parts and service, and finance and insurance products. The company also provides indirect financing through its captive finance company on vehicles sold.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on expanding its AutoNation USA used vehicle stores and enhancing its digital capabilities to improve customer experience and operational efficiency. Additionally, AutoNation is evaluating the impact of recent economic trends on customer behavior and adjusting its strategies accordingly.

SEC Filing: AUTONATION, INC. [ AN ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: Target to Cut Around 1,000 Jobs — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Nassauer

Target plans to cut around 1,800 corporate roles as part of an effort to remake its strategy to reverse a period of stagnant sales.

Target plans Thursday to announce it will lay off around 1,000 global corporate employees and cut 800 open roles, the company said. The move will cut around 8% of its approximately 22,000 corporate employees, with around 80% of the cuts in its U.S. ranks.

"The truth is, the complexity we've created over time has been holding us back," Michael Fiddelke, Target's incoming chief executive, said in a memo that will be sent to staff. "Too many layers and overlapping work have slowed decisions, making it harder to bring ideas to life."

The company will share details of the changes next Tuesday and plans to ask all U.S. corporate employees to work from home next week, he said in the planned memo. "It will be difficult," said Fiddelke. "It's a necessary step."

Target has reported 11 consecutive quarters of falling or weak comparable sales growth and named Fiddelke as its next CEO over the summer.

Fiddelke, who has spent his career at Target, will officially take the role in February, but in August said he was already working to speed up the pace of improvements, including assessing the company's headquarters structure, and refreshing the retailer's sharp focus on design.

Fiddelke wants to improve customers' shopping experience and speed up the adoption of technology inside the company, but style and design are "our North Star," said in an interview at the company's downtown Minneapolis office in August. "Target is being its most Target when those things are true," Fiddelke said.

The company's stock dropped 6.3% the day Fiddelke was announced as the retailer's next CEO and that longtime CEO Brian Cornell would stay on as executive chairman of Target's board, as many investors hoped for an outside candidate as change agent.

Managers will be affected at a higher rate than individual corporate employees, said a Target spokesman. Affected employees are being paid through January 3, in addition to potential severance payouts, he said.

Target reports quarterly earnings next month.

Write to Sarah Nassauer at Sarah.Nassauer@wsj.com

data.title: SAIC Names Interim CEO; Backs Fiscal Year Guidance Despite Government Shutdown
data.description:
By Josée Rose

Science Applications International Corporation said Toni Townes-Whitley is stepping down as chief executive, effective immediately.

The company named James Reagan as interim CEO.

SAIC, which provides information technology, engineering and technical services to the U.S. government, also backed its 2026 guidance, though it said a prolonged government shutdown may impair their ability to achieve that guidance. The company note that it reserves the right to reassess depending on when the shutdown ends and the broader government contracting environment.

SAIC said Reagan, who has served on the SAIC board since 2023, most recently was finance chief at Leidos Holdings, and previously was chief financial officer at Vencore, PAE and Aspect Communications. He also had senior roles at MCI Telecommunications, Nextel and AMS.

SAIC said its board now has 11 directors, 10 of whom are independent.

Write to Josée Rose at josee.rose@wsj.com

Magma Silver Announces Closing of $5 Million Private Placement
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - October 23, 2025) - Magma Silver Corp. (OTCQB: MAGMF) (FSE: BC21) (WKN: A411DV) (the "Company" or "Magma") is pleased to announce that, further to its news releases dated October 7 and 8, 2025, it has closed its non-brokered private placement (the "Offering") for aggregate gross proceeds of $5,000,000, through the issuance of 33,333,332 units of the Company (the "Units") at a price of $0.15 per Unit.

Each Unit consists of one common share of the Company (a "Common Share") and one-half of one common share purchase warrant (each whole warrant, a "Warrant"). Each Warrant entitles the holder to acquire one Common Share at an exercise price of $0.25 per Common Share for a period of 36 months following closing.

Eric Sprott, through 2176423 Ontario Ltd., a corporation beneficially owned by him, acquired 6,666,667 Units pursuant to the Offering for total consideration of $1,000,000. Prior to the Offering, Mr. Sprott did not beneficially own any securities.

As a result of the Offering, Mr. Sprott now beneficially owns 6,666,667 Shares and 3.333,334 Warrants (collectively, the "Sprott Securities"), representing approximately 9.5% of the outstanding Common Shares on a non-diluted basis and 13.6% of the outstanding Common Shares on a partially-diluted basis assuming exercise of such Warrants.

Mr. Sprott holds the Sprott Securities for investment purposes. Mr. Sprott has a long-term view of the investment and may acquire additional securities including on the open market or through private acquisitions or sell Magma securities including on the open market or through private dispositions in the future depending on market conditions, reformulation of plans and/or other relevant factors.

A copy of the early warning report with respect to the foregoing will appear shortly on Magma profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca and may also be obtained by calling Mr. Sprott's office at (416) 945-3294 (2176423 Ontario Ltd., 7 King Street East, Suite 1106, Toronto Ontario M5C 3C5).

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the Offering for exploration at the Niñobamba silver gold project in Peru, working capital and general corporate purposes.

In connection with the Offering, the Company paid aggregate cash finder's fees of $294,022 and issued an aggregate of 1,928,138 warrants (the "Finder Warrants") to certain eligible finders for their efforts in placing subscriptions under the Offering. Each Finder Warrant entitles the holder thereof to purchase one Common Shares at a price of $0.25 for a period of 36 months following the issuance of the Finder Warrants.

The Common Shares, Warrants, and Finder Warrants are subject to a statutory hold period in Canada of four (4) months and one (1) day after the closing.

The closing involved the issuance of an aggregate of 100,067 Units to directors, officers and insiders of the Company for gross proceeds of $15,010. Accordingly, the issuance of such securities (collectively, the "Insider Participation") constituted "related party transactions" within the meaning of Multilateral Instrument 61-101 Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions ("MI 61-101"). The Company was exempt from the requirements to obtain a formal valuation and minority shareholder approval in connection with the Insider Participation in reliance on sections 5.5(a) and 5.7(1)(a) of MI 61-101, as neither the fair market value of the Insider Participation nor the securities issued in connection therewith exceeded 25% of the Company's market capitalization.

Equity Incentive Awards

The Company also announces that its board of directors has approved the grant of a total of 3,525,000 incentive stock options (the "Stock Options") to certain of its directors, officers, consultants, and employees pursuant to the Company's Stock Option Plan. Such Stock Options are exercisable into Common Shares at an exercise price of $0.25 per share for a period of 5 years from the date of grant. The Stock Options vest in four equal tranches every three months, commencing on the date that is three months from the grant date. All Common Shares issued upon the exercise of the Stock Options will be subject to a hold period in Canada of four (4) months and one (1) day after the date of grant.

This press release is not an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy the securities in the United States or in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to qualification or registration under the securities laws of such jurisdiction. The securities being offered have not been, nor will they be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and such securities may not be offered or sold within the United States or to, or for the account or benefit of, U.S. persons absent registration or an applicable exemption from U.S. registration requirements and applicable U.S. state securities laws.

About Magma Silver Corp.

Magma Silver Corp. is a natural resources exploration company with a focus on acquiring, exploring, developing, and operating precious metal mining projects. Listed on the TSX Venture Exchange ("MGMA"), OTCQB ("MAGMF"), and Frankfurt Exchange ("BC21"), the Company's primary focus is on exploring and developing the advanced Niñobamba silver-gold project in the mining-friendly country of Peru. Niñobamba spans an 8 km mineralized corridor in a prolific geological belt of a high-sulphidation epithermal system. Extensive exploration by Newmont Corporation, AngloGold Ashanti Limited, Bear Creek Mining Corporation and others, has demonstrated significant resource potential with over C$14.5 million invested to date. Magma Silver is advancing its Niñobamba project using modern geological modelling and a strategic development plan. The property has excellent infrastructure, strong relationships with local communities and a regional technical team with over 50 years of mining experience in Peru.

For more information, please visit our website at www.magmasilver.com.

Contact Information

data.title: Federated Hermes to Buy Majority Stake in Real Estate Investment Manager FCP
data.description:
By Freddy Sebastian

Federated Hermes will take a majority stake in FCP Fund Manager, a private real estate investment manager, for up to $331 million.

The asset manager said Thursday that FCP had client assets of $3.8 billion as of June 30 and the deal for 80% of the firm includes $215.8 million in cash and $23.2 million in Class B shares that will be paid and issued at closing.

The agreement also includes contingent payments of up to $92 million over multiple years after the acquisition closes.

"Upon closing, this transaction will allow Federated Hermes to enter the U.S. Real Estate Market at a time when the Multifamily sector enjoys strong fundamentals and significant growth opportunities," Federated Hermes CEO J. Christopher Donahue said.

The deal, which is the company's second private markets acquisition this year, is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

In April, Federated Hermes acquired Rivington Energy Management, a U.K.-based infrastructure developer.

Write to Freddy Sebastian at freddy.sebastian@wsj.com

data.title: Soybean barge bids pull back slightly
data.description:
Basis bids for soybeans shipped by barge to U.S. Gulf Coast terminals pulled back a bit on Thursday after jumping on Wednesday, traders said.

  • Traders effectively dismissed a rumor that China had bought U.S. soybeans.

  • China, the world's top soy importer, has been absent from the U.S. market due to its trade conflict with Washington and has shifted its buying to South America.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump will meet Chinese President Xi Jinping next week, the White House said. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng will meet in Malaysia days before.

  • Traders were hopeful for a trade deal after U.S. farmers have pressured Trump over the loss of their biggest market.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture was not issuing daily or weekly export sales reports due to the federal government shutdown. This meant that traders would not receive an official confirmation of large sales even if they happened.

  • Analysts estimated U.S. soybean export sales were 600,000 to 2 million metric tons for the week ended on October 16 and that U.S. corn export sales were 800,000 to 2 million metric tons, according to a Reuters poll.

  • CIF basis bids for October soybean barges were down 2 cents at about 88 cents over Chicago Board of Trade November (SX25) futures, after rising on Wednesday. November barges were also bid at 88 cents over futures, down about 2 cents.

  • FOB export premiums for November-loaded soybeans were about 100 cents over futures.

  • CIF corn basis bids for October barges remained at about 82 cents over CBOT December (CZ25) futures. November barge loadings were bid at about 79 cents over futures, down 2 cents.

  • FOB export premiums for October corn shipments were about 93 cents over futures.

data.title: Anthropic to expand use of Google Cloud's TPU chips
data.description: Anthropic on Thursday said it would expand the use of Google Cloud's TPU chips, providing the artificial intelligence startup access to the capacity and computing resources required to train the next generations of Claude models.
